teacher: Matsunami Goro (Hero name: 'Scaffold' — Quirk: Construct, builds rigid scaffolding structures from ambient minerals. Methodical, patient, and quietly competitive about his class.)
reputation: The class that almost made it to 1-A. Their entrance exam scores were comparable — in some technical categories, better. Matsunami runs a tighter ship than Oshiro. 1-B students know their fundamentals cold before they start improvising. Less chaos, more precision. They watch 1-A the way you watch someone doing something impressive badly.
sports_festival_note: Tier 2, Arc 1. Class 1-B enter the Sports Festival as the school's open secret — everyone's picked a 1-A favourite, nobody's thought hard about 1-B. That's exactly how Matsunami wants it. He has drilled his students on 1-A's quirks from the entrance exam footage. This is the arc where 1-B stops being background noise.
dm_overview: Introduce 1-B students gradually through Tier 1 — brief corridor encounters, overheard conversations, cafeteria sightings. None of them are villains. None of them are friends yet. They're rivals in the classical sense: people who make your students better by existing. By the time the Sports Festival starts, players should have a clear read on at least 3-4 of these students and be genuinely unsure who's going to win.

tier1_introduction_guide.arc1_entrance_exam: The results board after the exam shows Class 1-A and 1-B scores in adjacent columns. 1-B's top score (Morida: 84 pts) is visible. Whether it beats or matches 1-A's best depends on the session. Fujisaki mentions '1-B had a different site this year — quieter, but thorough.' Oshiro says nothing about 1-B. He doesn't need to.
tier1_introduction_guide.arc2_dorm_life: Morida and Yashiro are visible in the cafeteria. Morida's tray is geometrically arranged. He looks at the 1-A table once, says nothing. Yashiro catches the eye of whoever's watching and winks. The practice room booking shows 1-B has 6am slots every weekday. Ogiwara's warmup shakes the building at 7am. The class notices.
tier1_introduction_guide.arc3_capture_the_flag: Ogiwara is watching through the observation window. When a student looks over, he gives a slow thumbs up. It's impossible to tell if it's sincere or condescending. Oshiro drops the line: 'Class 1-B had three students who topped this score in their equivalent drill. That's your benchmark.'
tier1_introduction_guide.arc4_agency_visit: Kitamura Sora is at the same agency district for a different internship. If the class encounters her: she's carrying precisely folded documents, gives the impression she's been there three days, and has a 10-second corridor interaction before continuing. She doesn't introduce herself. Her name badge faces inward. If a student catches a glimpse: Kitamura, S., Class 1-B.

students.name: Morida Takeshi

quirk_mechanics.weakness: His hardened form can only absorb a limited amount of damage before it shatters, ending the effect and returning him to normal — any damage taken beyond his absorption limit carries over to his normal form.

abilities.name: Harden
abilities.description: Turns his skin to stone, gaining resistance to all damage types (except necrotic, psychic, and poison) for minutes equal to half his Constitution modifier. As a reaction, can move up to half his speed to intercept an incoming attack on an ally's behalf. Creatures within 10 ft directly behind him gain half cover.

abilities.name: Stone Weapon
abilities.description: Fists become blades (slashing) or solid stone fists (bludgeoning), dealing 1d10 + Strength modifier damage. Once per turn on a hit, deals bonus Xd4 damage (X = half Constitution modifier, rounded down). Gains +1 AC.

abilities.name: Re-Harden
abilities.description: If his Harden threshold is breached, regains 10 HP instantly; usable up to five times in one reaction. After using it, falls unconscious for rounds equal to the number of uses beyond the first. Once per short rest.

abilities.name: Unbreakable
abilities.description: Hardens his entire body for 4 rounds, becoming immune to all damage except necrotic, psychic, and poison, and granting total cover to allies directly behind him. Gains piercing damage and a climbing speed. Falls unconscious for 3 rounds and gains three levels of exhaustion afterward.

students.quirk_summary: Full-body metallic plating. Morida's skin hardens into dense, layered steel armour that he can extend or retract at will. At full hardening he's nearly impervious to physical attacks. At partial hardening he trades some protection for mobility. He cannot harden his eyes — his one consistent weak point.
students.personality: Disciplined, methodical, and quietly contemptuous of flashy quirk use. He respects results and is suspicious of anything he can't measure. Doesn't talk during a fight. Will compliment a student after if they earned it — which sounds like 'That was efficient.' He means it as high praise.
students.rivalry_dynamic: Engine vs Wall. Izuto's raw output vs Morida's near-absolute defence. The central question is whether Izuto can generate enough force to crack the armour before Morida just outlasts him. Morida has already figured out Izuto's timing. Izuto hasn't thought about Morida at all yet.
students.sports_festival_role: The unmovable object in direct contests. Morida's goal in every event is to simply not be eliminated. He doesn't need points — he needs to still be standing when everyone else isn't. He and Izuto will meet in the tournament bracket. That fight is the centrepiece of the Sports Festival arc.
students.tier1_appearance: Cafeteria during dorm move-in. Perfectly level tray. Makes eye contact with no one. Once, in the corridor, he passes Izuto without acknowledging him. Izuto doesn't notice. Morida does.
students.dm_notes: Morida is not a villain and not a bad person. He's the structural version of Masahata — someone who sees everything as a competition and is usually right about where everyone stands. Let him be correct. Let his correctness be slightly annoying. The payoff is at the Sports Festival when his certainty runs into Izuto's stubbornness.
signature_moves: Iron Stance: as a bonus action, increase AC by 2 until start of next turn (total AC 20). Speed drops to 0.
Armour Crash: full hardening bull rush — move 20ft in a line, +6 to hit, 2d8+3 bludgeoning, DC 14 STR save or knocked prone. Recharge 5-6.
Selective Hardening: can harden specific body parts — hardened fist deals +1d6 on a hit. Always on.

students.name: Yashiro Fumiko

quirk_mechanics.weakness: Part of her real body must be present to release the dark fog, exposing her to attack. Portals are coordinate-based, requiring her to know the exact location or have a clear line of sight, and she cannot create portals in heavy winds.

abilities.name: Portal Creation
abilities.description: Creates a number of 20-ft diameter portals up to her Proficiency Bonus within 30 feet, connecting to any location she can see or for which she has precise coordinates. Each portal lasts up to one minute or until dismissed. As a reaction, she can close a portal as a creature attempts to move through it — that creature makes a Dexterity save against her Quirk DC or takes 3d10 damage and is pushed back 10 feet.

abilities.name: Fog Shroud
abilities.description: Envelops herself in dark fog, becoming heavily obscured for one minute. While shrouded she can create portals as a bonus action, and attacks against her while obscured have disadvantage.

students.quirk_summary: Short-range teleportation. Yashiro can teleport up to 10 ft in any direction as a free action. Longer jumps (up to 30ft) require a bonus action and a 3-round cooldown. She can't blink through solid objects but can blink around corners she can see around. She uses this continuously and casually, which is deeply disorienting to fight.
students.personality: Smug, playful, treats combat like a game she's already won. Finds 1-A's chaos endearing in the way that people find puppies endearing — they're trying so hard. Genuinely likes competition. Would rather lose a good fight than win a boring one. Her competitive sincerity is the thing that catches people off guard — they expect cheap tricks and get instead a student who is actually, seriously fast.
students.rivalry_dynamic: Speed vs Evasion. Ren is faster in a straight line. Yashiro can't be chased in one. The question is whether Ren can close to a range where his attacks land before she blinks out. She's studied his Overclock pattern. He doesn't know she exists yet.
students.sports_festival_role: The worst person to be paired against in any containment event — she just isn't there. She's going to be in the tournament bracket and her match against Ren is the one that players will talk about afterwards regardless of who wins.
students.tier1_appearance: First spotted vanishing around a corner that didn't have a turn in it. If a student catches her eye in the cafeteria, she winks and blinks to three seats over without using the gap.
students.dm_notes: Yashiro is genuinely fun to run. She should feel slippery and quick, not cheap. There's always a clear solution to fighting her — the 3-round cooldown on long jumps, the can't-blink-through-solids rule — but finding it under pressure is hard. She and Ren actually like each other by the end of the Festival. Neither admits it.
signature_moves: Blink Step (Free): teleport up to 10 ft as part of movement. Can do this each turn without cost.
Long Jump (Bonus action): teleport up to 30ft. 3-round cooldown.
Phantom Strike: teleports adjacent to a target, attacks, then blinks 10ft away as part of the same action. Doesn't provoke opportunity attacks from that target.

students.name: Denma Sora

quirk_mechanics.weakness: His fingers must be exposed to use the Quirk, and prolonged use risks collateral damage — a failed save against Black Hole within 10 feet of fragile structures may cause them to collapse. While his fingers are exposed, ranged attacks against him within 10 feet have disadvantage and loose objects under 50 pounds drift toward him.

abilities.name: Black Hole
abilities.description: Creates a gravitational pulse in a 30-ft cone. Each creature makes a Strength save against his Quirk DC, taking 2d8 force damage and being pulled 15 feet closer on a fail, or half damage on a success. A creature ending its turn within 5 feet of him after a failed save becomes Restrained, taking 2d8 force damage at the start of each turn until it saves again.

abilities.name: Singularity Vortex
abilities.description: Creates a miniature black hole at a point within 60 feet for 1 minute. Each creature starting its turn within 15 feet makes a Strength save or is pulled 10 feet closer and takes 6d8 force damage (half on success). The area is difficult terrain and ranged attacks through it have disadvantage. Can be moved up to 20 feet as a bonus action.

abilities.name: Micro Collapse
abilities.description: Can disintegrate up to 50 pounds of non-living material (metal, stone, wood) by focusing the black hole, useful for disarming or destroying small unattended items or weakened constructs.

students.quirk_summary: Creates localised gravitational anomalies. Denma can designate a 10ft sphere within 60ft as either a high-gravity zone (things pulled toward its centre) or a low-gravity zone (things float away from it). Maintaining a zone requires concentration. He can have two zones active simultaneously at full concentration, but cannot attack while doing so.
students.personality: Quiet. Unsettling. Speaks in short declarative sentences and rarely finishes thoughts that the other person can follow anyway. Not cold — just somewhere else most of the time. Will talk at length about gravitational physics and the difference between mass and weight in a way that is technically correct and socially obliterating. The moments where he's actually paying attention to a person are rare and feel significant.
students.rivalry_dynamic: Both manipulate the battlefield rather than dealing direct damage. Chosen builds stress, breaks people down. Denma controls movement, removes options. Their rivalry is philosophical more than physical — two students who win through mechanism rather than power. At the Festival: both will try to make the other fight on their terms. Neither will fully succeed.
students.sports_festival_role: The wildcard in every group event. His zones control the field in ways that benefit teammates and punish enemies. In the tournament bracket, he's dangerous specifically because he doesn't need to beat you — he just needs you to be in the wrong place. Almost everyone underestimates the range.
students.tier1_appearance: A practice room booking under 1-B's name. Inside: twelve thumbtacks floating 2mm off the board, perfectly static. No explanation. The room smells faintly of ozone.
students.dm_notes: Denma is designed to be memorable through strangeness rather than threat. His encounters in Tier 1 should be brief and odd — a glance, a sentence, something not quite right about gravity in his vicinity. Save his actual fighting capability for the Festival. He should feel like a puzzle that hasn't been asked yet.
signature_moves: Gravity Zone (Action): designate a 10ft sphere within 60ft as high-gravity (creatures pulled to centre, speed halved) or low-gravity (creatures float up 5ft, speed halved in different axis). Concentration, 1 minute.
Dual Field: maintain two zones simultaneously. Cannot attack or take bonus actions while sustaining both.
Crush Point: collapse a high-gravity zone onto a single target. DC 14 STR save or restrained for 1 round and takes 2d6 force damage. Destroys the zone.

students.name: Ogiwara Bunta

quirk_mechanics.weakness: Prolonged use strains his muscles and joints — after using Big Fist a number of times equal to his level times his proficiency bonus, he must make a DC 13 Constitution save or take 1d12 true damage.

abilities.name: Giant Strength
abilities.description: Strength score maximum becomes 22, and carrying capacity is doubled.

abilities.name: Giant Punch
abilities.description: Enlarges a hand and strikes all creatures in a 10-ft long, 10-ft wide line, dealing 2d12 bludgeoning damage and producing a wind shock that pushes targets back 15 feet.

abilities.name: Ground Pound
abilities.description: Slams an enlarged hand into the ground. All creatures within a 20-ft radius make a Dexterity save or take 4d12 bludgeoning damage and are knocked prone; the area becomes difficult terrain.

students.quirk_summary: Ogiwara can enlarge either hand to enormous size, vastly increasing his striking power and reach. A straight punch becomes a battering ram that flattens anything in a line; slammed into the ground it sends a shockwave of displaced air and debris radiating outward. Scaling: a standard Giant Punch hits everything in a 10-ft line. A full Ground Pound clears a 20-ft radius and leaves the area difficult terrain. Doesn't work if his hand can't reach the target or the ground.
students.personality: Loud. Genuinely, physically loud — his voice carries without him trying. Enthusiastic competitor in the way that makes it fun to lose to him because he's so happy about having a real contest. No subtlety. No hidden agenda. Will shake your hand and mean it. Will also absolutely try to put you through the floor.
students.rivalry_dynamic: Sound vs Big Fist. Both create broad-area chaos. Both are physically enormous and running at things is their default strategy. They'll either be best friends or destroy each other immediately at the Festival — the exact circumstances of their first meeting will determine which. Either outcome is correct.
students.sports_festival_role: Devastating in group events that require movement or terrain control. In the tournament bracket, he's the crowd favourite — his Giant Punch and Ground Pound create visible, dramatic shockwaves that the audience loves. Weakness: if someone gets him airborne or out of arm's reach, his quirk is useless. Not a weakness he's addressed.
students.tier1_appearance: Two 7am shockwaves on move-in morning, felt through the dorm floor. If confronted: 'Warmup.' Delivered as a complete explanation. Gives the thumbs up through the Capture the Flag observation window — entirely genuine, completely unreadable.
students.dm_notes: Ogiwara is the relief. Run every Tier 1 scene with him as a moment of levity. He has no secrets, no agenda, no hidden depth to excavate. His arc at the Sports Festival is uncomplicated: he wants to test himself against Toshida. Give him that fight and let it be excellent.
signature_moves: Giant Punch (Scaled): enlarges a hand and strikes everything in a 10-ft line. 2d10 bludgeoning damage and a wind-shock push of 10 feet.
Ground Pound (Scaled): slams an enlarged hand into the ground. 15-ft radius, 3d10 bludgeoning, DC 14 DEX save or knocked prone. Difficult terrain left behind. Recharge 5-6.
Giant Strength (Passive): his Strength score maximum is treated as 22, and his carrying capacity is doubled.

students.name: Nashiro Aoi

quirk_mechanics.weakness: Physical contact is required to copy a Quirk, and each copied power fades after two minutes. Accumulation- or mutation-based Quirks cannot be properly replicated, and upon copying she inherits all of its drawbacks, requiring a successful DC 16 Athletics check — on a failure, she cannot attempt to copy that specific Quirk again for seven rounds.

abilities.name: Mimic Touch
abilities.description: When she lands an unarmed strike on a creature, she may copy their Quirk. The copied Quirk lasts two minutes (three at 5th level, five at 10th level).

abilities.name: Quirk Swap
abilities.description: Switches between stored copied Quirks. She may maintain up to two copied Quirks at once, increasing by one additional Quirk at 5th and 10th level.

students.quirk_summary: Nashiro can borrow a Quirk by landing a hit on its user — for two minutes she has full use of whatever ability they're carrying, layered over (or instead of) her own approach to a fight. She's spent years cataloguing how other people's powers actually work in practice, which means she rarely copies something she doesn't already know how to use well. She cannot copy mutation- or accumulation-based Quirks, and copying anything risky means inheriting its drawbacks too.
students.personality: Theatrical. Observant in the way that only people who spend a lot of time watching other people are. In public she's performing — always, slightly, a version of herself edited for the room. In private (which almost never happens with 1-A) she's dry and tired in an interesting way. She's been reading Class 1-A since the results board, cataloguing every Quirk she's seen on tape and deciding which would actually be worth taking from them in a fight.
students.rivalry_dynamic: The obvious play for Nashiro against Hellflame is to land a hit and copy it — Kazama knows this, which means the real fight is about whether she can tag him before he adjusts his approach to deny her the touch. If she copies his fire, the fight changes entirely on her terms. Kazama doesn't know she exists yet. Their Sports Festival match, if it happens, is about whether Nashiro can land that first hit before Kazama figures out what she's doing.
students.sports_festival_role: Nightmarish in group events that involve direct confrontation — she can absorb whoever hits her first and turn their own Quirk against the rest of the field. In the tournament bracket, she's a puzzle encounter: students need to avoid giving her a clean touch while she's actively hunting for the most dangerous Quirk on the field to borrow.
students.tier1_appearance: A 1-A student swears Hamagawa tagged them with a light shove in a sparring drill, then suddenly fought like Hamagawa for thirty seconds. It wasn't Hamagawa. Nobody can prove it was Nashiro. If directly confronted: 'I don't know what you're referring to.' She says it with Hamagawa's exact dry cadence.
students.dm_notes: Nashiro is a slow-burn reveal. Her Tier 1 appearances should all be deniable — a tagged opponent briefly fighting unlike themselves, easy to write off as imagination. By the Sports Festival, players should be actively wary of letting her land a hit, which is exactly where she wants them.
signature_moves: Mimic Touch (Bonus action): on landing an unarmed strike, copy the target's Quirk. Lasts two minutes. Cannot copy mutation- or accumulation-based Quirks, and inherits the copied Quirk's drawbacks.
Quirk Swap (Bonus action): switches which copied Quirk is currently active. Can hold up to two copied Quirks in reserve at once.
Cold Read (Passive): from prior observation of an opponent's Quirk in use, she has advantage on the Athletics check required when first copying it.

students.name: Kitamura Sora

quirk_mechanics.weakness: Tape is durable but not unbreakable. She can produce tape for up to 10 minutes of use; after that, she cannot generate more until she takes a short rest to recover from the resulting dry skin.

abilities.name: Grapple and Restrain
abilities.description: Shoots tape at a target within 60 feet. On a hit, the target becomes grappled and restrained, with a Strength save against her Quirk DC at the end of its turn to break free. While grappled, she can move the creature left or right up to 30 feet.

abilities.name: Throwing Objects
abilities.description: Attaches tape to up to two objects and throws them, making two separate attacks — each object deals bludgeoning damage based on its size per the Object Size table (e.g., Medium objects 1d8, Large objects 2d8).

abilities.name: Rescue Maneuver
abilities.description: Shoots tape at a grappled ally, pulling them toward her and allowing her to move up to 60 feet with them to reposition or avoid attacks.

abilities.name: Barricade Tape
abilities.description: Rapidly ejects tape to cover up to a 10-ft square radius 60 feet away, creating a sticky barrier that provides half cover to allies and halves the speed of creatures moving through it.

students.quirk_summary: Generates strong, adhesive tape from her forearms. She can fire it to bind a target at range, sling tape-wrapped objects as improvised weapons, throw a tether to pull an ally clear of danger, or flood an area with sticky barricade tape that bogs down anyone moving through it. Setting up a barricade or restraint line in a room takes her approximately six seconds. She has timed this.
students.personality: Precise. Economical. Considers Class 1-A's operational style inefficient in the way an architect considers a building that keeps falling down. She isn't contemptuous — she's concerned, professionally. Has extremely strong opinions about workspace organisation and does not volunteer them unsolicited. Will volunteer them if asked. Has very few opinions about things she considers not her domain. Says 'Yes.' and 'No.' as complete sentences.
students.rivalry_dynamic: Size vs Tape. Nakaya's enlargement makes her a large target — and a large target takes a lot more tape to bring down, but once Kitamura's got enough of it on her, the restraint scales with the problem. Nakaya's advantage is that at sufficient size, she can simply tear through tape that would hold a normal-sized opponent — but that takes time and effort Kitamura can use against her. Their rivalry is about Nakaya learning that raw scale has hard limits.
students.sports_festival_role: Devastating in any event with structure — she pre-rigs environments with barricade tape during setup phases. In group contests, she's invisible until suddenly four students are bound simultaneously. In the tournament bracket, she's the student everyone is afraid to face because they've seen her work and don't know where the tape is.
students.tier1_appearance: Corridor encounter during the Agency Visit arc. 10 seconds. She's carrying precisely folded documents, doesn't introduce herself, continues walking. Her name badge faces inward. If a student catches a glimpse: Kitamura, S., 1-B. The practice room she used before leaving has tape patterns still strung across it at odd angles — easy to miss until someone almost walks into one.
students.dm_notes: Kitamura is the student who makes players feel like they've been outplayed by something they didn't see coming. Her Tier 1 presence should be minimal and precise — the corridor encounter, the tape patterns. Nothing else. By the Sports Festival, her reputation has been built entirely by absence. The fight against her should be the one that makes players think hard about the environment before they enter it.
signature_moves: Barricade Tape (Reaction): ejects tape to cover a 10-ft square up to 60ft away, creating a sticky barrier that grants allies half cover and halves the speed of creatures moving through it.
Grapple and Restrain (Action): shoots tape at a target within 60ft. On a hit, the target is grappled and restrained, with a Strength save at the end of its turn to break free.
Rescue Maneuver (Reaction): shoots tape at a grappled ally, pulling them toward her and repositioning up to 60 feet to get them clear of danger.
